## Purpose
This file exists to outline a refactor plan for DiffDoc, aimed at improving its scalability across large codebases by implementing a new architecture for generating and storing summaries.

## User-Visible Behavior
Users will interact with the CLI commands `summarize` and `embed`, which will generate and manage summary files in a structured manner. Users will see updates in the manifest and summary files based on their commands, with error reporting for any issues encountered during processing.

## Business Rules
- The refactor introduces a breaking schema change with no migration path from the old format.
- Summaries are stored as independent JSON files, and the manifest maps source files to their content hashes.
- The system will continue processing files even if some fail, reporting errors at the end.
- No concurrency control is implemented initially, and the `embed` command will retain its name.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
Data inputs include source files, command-line arguments, and configuration settings. Outputs consist of JSON summary files and a manifest file that tracks file hashes.

## Side Effects
The refactor will create new directories and files under `.diffdoc/`, including manifest and summary files. It will also modify the existing manifest based on file changes and may delete orphaned summary files.

## Error And Edge Cases
The system will handle errors by collecting per-file summarize errors and continuing processing. It will exit with a non-zero status if any failures occur. Edge cases include handling unchanged files, deleted paths, and duplicate-content files.

## Dependencies
No external or internal dependencies are identified in this file.

## Operational Notes
The implementation will require careful attention to atomic writes for manifest and summary files to ensure data integrity. The system is designed to be crash-safe and will utilize temporary files for writes. The refactor will also necessitate updates to documentation to reflect the new architecture.
